_doc: Microsoft Fluent Slide System — PATTERN REGISTRY. Searchable index of reusable slide+motion patterns keyed by INTENT. Each entry says: what story beat it tells, what to reach for, the markup contract, and where it's proven in the wild. Search with: node patterns.js find <keyword>. Add with: node patterns.js add (or hand-edit this file). When you build a NEW slide archetype with a distinct motion, register it here so it can be searched and reused later.

_schema.intent: the STORY BEAT this pattern serves — what the slide is trying to say (this is what you search on)
_schema.when: trigger conditions — when to reach for this
_schema.archetype: template type in build.js / templates.js (or 'custom' if hand-built)
_schema.markup: the minimal markup contract to instantiate it
_schema.proven_in: deck + slide where it shipped and was visually verified

patterns.intent: Show the REAL product diagram/chart/screenshot from a referenced source page, instead of describing it in text or hand-drawing an approximation. The single best asset for 'how X works' is usually already on the announcement page — embed it as a framed exhibit.
patterns.when: A slide references a source link (announcement/blog/docs) whose page contains a diagram, architecture, benchmark chart, or product screenshot that captures the point better than words. Run link-image-audit.py on the deck's links, vision-inspect candidates, embed the good one.
patterns.motion: static (r reveal on header)
patterns.markup: T.webexhibit({kicker,title,img,plate:'white'|'none',fit:'contain'|'cover',caption,src:{label,url},alt,dark}) — img = path under assets/img OR data: URI OR http URL. ALWAYS pass src.url for provenance.
patterns.proven_in: webexhibit-demo (MDASH lifecycle diagram from Microsoft Security blog, white plate on dark slide, frame-verified no-clip)
patterns.pitfalls: GEOMETRY: the plate is position:absolute;inset:0 inside a position:relative stage (flex:1 1 0) — do NOT use a shrink-wrap inline-flex plate with img max-height:100%; the flex max-height chain won't bind and the image overflows top/bottom (this exact clip bug shipped twice before the absolute-fill fix). PLATE: white plate for light/transparent diagrams on a dark deck (never recolor the source art — same contrast-plate rule as logos); plate:'none' for images already dark/full-bleed. SOURCING: real images only, with provenance (src.url). Base64-embed or copy into assets/img so the deck stays self-contained. VISION-INSPECT before embedding: the og:image is often a decorative hero, NOT the explanatory diagram — pick by looking, not by rank.

patterns.intent: Open a deck with authority — big editorial title over a photographic duotone, presenter identity, no clutter.
patterns.when: First slide of any briefing/keynote recap.
patterns.motion: r (staggered reveal of eyebrow→title→subtitle→meta)
patterns.markup: T.cover({img, eyebrow, title, subtitle, meta:[{k,v}], badge})
patterns.pitfalls: Keep title <= 19ch or it wraps past the photo focal point. No 'Confidential/NDA' footer on customer-facing decks.

patterns.intent: State the core shift as a two-card left→right contrast: 'Until now X' → 'Now Y'. The horizontal order IS the argument.
patterns.when: Slide 2 of a recap — frame the one shift everything else hangs on.
patterns.motion: r with staggered --d: left card .35s, arrow .9s, right card 1.25s (left must reveal first)
patterns.markup:
…
→
…
patterns.pitfalls: Don't append a summary caption under it — let the contrast stand (removed one that read AI-generated). Stagger delays must make LEFT land before RIGHT.

patterns.intent: Dramatize 'from answering to doing': the OLD way (a paragraph you still execute by hand) appears first and holds, then the AGENT panel takes over and ticks the work done itself.
patterns.when: Show a concrete workflow the agent now owns end-to-end.
patterns.motion: seq-recede (left: reveal→hold→recede in ONE keyframe) + seq-takeover (right at 1.6s) + seq-task ticks + draw-check
patterns.markup:
…paragraph…
…
×N
patterns.pitfalls: GUARD-RAIL #1: the recede panel must NOT also carry .r — one self-contained keyframe only, or it reveals late and out of order.

patterns.intent: Lay out the run-of-show as accent-numbered rows; the active section can be highlighted.
patterns.when: Right after the thesis — tell them the three (or N) moves.
patterns.motion: r staggered per row (--accent sets the per-row color)
patterns.markup: T.agenda({title, items:[{n,t,d,active}]})
patterns.pitfalls: Cap at 3-4 rows; this is a map, not the territory.

patterns.intent: Chapter break — giant section number + theme title over a duotone, so the audience feels the gear-change.
patterns.when: Between major themes.
patterns.motion: r (eyebrow→title→sub→tags); giant numeral is static accent
patterns.markup: T.section({num, img, eyebrow, title, subtitle, tags})
patterns.pitfalls: Keep the same numeral style across all dividers for rhythm.

patterns.intent: Argue 'your moat is your context, not the model': scattered enterprise sources fly IN and fuse into one governed agent orb.
patterns.when: Make the case that connected proprietary data — not model size — is the differentiator.
patterns.motion: converge (cchip fly-in from edges to rest ring) + cvorb fuse; motion-cap payoff line
patterns.markup: One governed agent, grounded in your data.
patterns.pitfalls: GUARD-RAIL #2: caption uses margin-left centering (transform-free), never translateX(-50%) + rise.

patterns.intent: Show N grounding sources feeding ONE hub — labeled nodes around a center, dashed streams flowing inward, each label pinned to its stream's dot.
patterns.when: A hub-and-spoke product family (e.g. the IQ surfaces) that all feed one agent.
patterns.motion: iqsvg path flow + fadein staggered; iqdot anchors; iqhub fuse+spin; motion-cap payoff
patterns.markup: Full-stage <svg viewBox='0 0 1280 720'> with <path> streams + <circle class='iqdot'> at each label; HTML labels positioned to match dot coords.
patterns.pitfalls: ALIGNMENT: SVG viewBox must equal slide size (1280×720) and fill 1:1 so SVG coords == px coords; put dots INSIDE the svg, never in HTML space. Payoff caption follows GUARD-RAIL #2.

patterns.intent: Make security/detection visceral: a grid field under a sweeping beam that surfaces threats as sequential 'hit' pings — defense moving at AI speed.
patterns.when: Any security, threat-detection, red-team, monitoring, or compliance-scan slide.
patterns.motion: scan .beam (continuous sweep) + scan .hit (sequential pop-in)
patterns.markup: ×N
patterns.pitfalls: Red accent reads as 'threat'; switch hit color to green if the story is 'all clear'. Beam loops infinitely — fine, it reads as always-on.

patterns.intent: Signal an always-on / monitoring / autopilot capability with a radiating pulse ring that never stops.
patterns.when: Autopilot, 24/7 agent, background monitoring, heartbeat.
patterns.motion: pulsering (two offset expanding rings, infinite)
patterns.pitfalls: One pulse per slide — multiple competing pulses look noisy.

patterns.intent: Show parallel agent work as a git-worktree: a trunk that forks into concurrent branches, each node waking in sequence.
patterns.when: Parallel/concurrent execution, multi-agent fan-out, branching workflows.
patterns.motion: tnode (sequential fade-in of branch endpoints)
patterns.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 360 400'> branch <path>s + <circle class='tnode'> at each node endpoint.
patterns.pitfalls: Nodes are <circle> INSIDE the SVG at exact path endpoints — never dots in pixel/HTML space over an SVG (they won't align).

patterns.intent: Let one number land — a giant figure (optionally counting up) + one line of meaning. Detail lives in the speaker's mouth.
patterns.when: A single proof point you want to hit hard (productivity %, multiplier, count).
patterns.motion: big-figure land (scale+fade); optional data-countup integer roll
patterns.markup: T.big({figure, line, kicker, img}) — for count-up: 0
patterns.pitfalls: Label illustrative/internal numbers as such — don't imply a customer's own result. Count-up needs the engine (data-countup hook).

patterns.intent: Itemize what changes across dimensions: de-carded before→after rows (anti-blocky), each with an icon and a from→to.
patterns.when: Multiple parallel before/after deltas (interaction, scope, workflow, context).
patterns.motion: r staggered per row
patterns.markup: T.compare({fromLabel, toLabel, rows:[{dim,icon,from,to}]})
patterns.pitfalls: Keep 'from' muted and 'to' emphasized so the eye lands on the new state.

patterns.intent: Introduce a single product/announcement: identity + headline + 'For <customer>' relevance on the left, a motion stage on the right.
patterns.when: Each headline product you want to land individually with a live visual.
patterns.motion: r staggered; right stage hosts any motion pattern (scanner, pulse, worktree)
patterns.markup:
…an-top/an-head/an-bank/an-link…
…motion…
patterns.pitfalls: Always carry a real source link; pair the right-stage motion to the product's meaning (security→scanner, parallel→worktree).

patterns.intent: Sweep up the rest of a theme's announcements as a tidy 4-card grid — breadth without a slide each.
patterns.when: You have 3-5 secondary items under a theme that don't each merit a full slide.
patterns.motion: r staggered per card
patterns.markup: grid of cards: {name, blurb, pill(GA/Preview/New)}
patterns.pitfalls: Verify every item is real and correctly statused (GA vs Preview vs pre-Build GA) — this grid is where padding/errors hide.

patterns.intent: Close on the ask: a short title + numbered next steps grounded in the customer's actual engagement.
patterns.when: Final slide — convert recap into committed next actions.
patterns.motion: r staggered; numbered steps in brand colors
patterns.markup: T.closing({title, steps:[{k,v}], img})
patterns.pitfalls: Ground steps in the real account context (use case, KPI, MVE) — generic 'scope a pilot' reads as filler.

patterns.intent: Sequence a rollout as a vertical timeline — when→what→outcome, so a plan feels inevitable.
patterns.when: 90-day plan, phased rollout, adoption roadmap.
patterns.motion: r staggered per tl-item
patterns.markup: T.timeline({title,body,items:[{when,t,b}]})
patterns.pitfalls: Lead with what is GA; stage previews behind it.

patterns.intent: Force a prioritisation decision: plot items on a 2x2 of impact vs feasibility, with the target quadrant visually emphasized so the eye lands on what to invest in.
patterns.when: Workshop prioritisation, portfolio triage, effort-vs-value sorting.
patterns.motion: mq quadrants stagger-reveal (r-style nth-child delays)
patterns.markup:
Y-axis
..
..
..
x4
X-axis
patterns.pitfalls: Rotate Y-axis label with transform:rotate(-90deg) in a fixed-width gutter so it never overlaps the quadrants. Emphasize ONE target quadrant (green gradient + star), mute the rest.

patterns.intent: Show an ordered capability/complexity ladder: N color-coded tiers from simplest to hardest, each with a name, a one-line definition, and a concrete example.
patterns.when: Maturity model, agent/data complexity levels, build-difficulty tiers, crawl-walk-run.
patterns.motion: lv rows stagger-reveal top-to-bottom
patterns.markup:
Level Ntag
definition
example
patterns.pitfalls: Left-border color carries the tier identity; keep the 3-column grid (name|definition|example) aligned so rows scan as a table.

patterns.intent: Map a process across sequential steps against fixed dimensions: a grid with dimension rows down the left and step columns across, one worked example column showing the depth expected and the rest blank for participants to fill.
patterns.when: As-is/to-be journey mapping, service blueprint, process audit, workshop fill-in canvas.
patterns.motion: grid reveals as one block (r on .jr)
patterns.markup: 5 cols x N rows: jr-head row, then per dimension: jr-dim (label+sub) | jr-ex (worked example) | jr-blank x3
patterns.pitfalls: Give blank participant cells a faint diagonal-hatch background so they read as fill-me-in, not broken. Keep example-cell font ~12.5px so multi-line content never overflows the cell.

patterns.intent: Present a library of reusable building blocks grouped into color-coded families, as a scannable multi-column catalog — the menu participants pick from.
patterns.when: Capability catalog, component/pattern library, agent/card decks, a menu of options grouped by type.
patterns.motion: catcol columns stagger-reveal left-to-right
patterns.markup:
Family
item
..
x3
patterns.pitfalls: Balance items across columns so no column overflows the slide bottom; family color = left-border + header dot. Add a legend row if items carry a type (e.g. Retrieval vs Task).

patterns.intent: Teach an activity: numbered do-this steps on the left, a worked example or template panel on the right — the standard how-to layout for a workshop instruction slide.
patterns.when: Any workshop/training step that tells participants exactly what to do, paired with an example.
patterns.motion: timebox + title + do rows stagger; example panel reveals alongside
patterns.markup:
timebox + s-title + s-q
N
stepdetail
worked example / ex-card
patterns.pitfalls: Pair every instruction with a concrete example (ex-card / template) — abstract steps alone read as filler. Carry a timebox badge so participants know the pace.

patterns.intent: Show the shape of something at a glance as unequal tiles — a dominant HERO tile carries the single most important point, smaller tiles supply supporting stats/context around it. The size hierarchy IS the editorial decision about what matters most.
patterns.when: Quarter/program overview, product family, 'state of X' summary — when you want one thing to dominate and 3-4 things to support without equal weight.
patterns.motion: bt tiles stagger-reveal (rise, nth-child delays); hero tile reveals first
patterns.markup:
…big point…
stat
stat
…
— grid 4col×2row; .hero spans 2×2, .wide spans 2.
patterns.pitfalls: The hero tile must be the actual headline, not just the biggest box — size = importance. Balance smaller tiles so none overflow; keep hero body ≤34ch. Don't make all tiles equal (that's just a card grid). Run qa.js — bento tile collisions show as OVERLAP.

patterns.intent: Executive readout: N metric tiles, each a big number + QoQ delta (up/down, colored) + an inline sparkline trend. Lets execs scan health in 3 seconds.
patterns.when: Program health, business review, metrics summary, before/after a quarter. Any 'numbers that moved' slide.
patterns.motion: kp tiles stagger-reveal (rise, nth-child delays)
patterns.markup:
label
42
▲ +18 QoQ
{sparkline svg}
×N. Sparkline: build path d from data array, viewBox 160×30, preserveAspectRatio=none.
patterns.pitfalls: Delta color must match direction semantics (green up = good, but red down can be GOOD for 'blockers' — set per metric, not per arrow). Sparkline normalizes to its own min/max so a flat series still reads; guard mx==mn divide-by-zero. 3×2 grid max before tiles get cramped.

patterns.intent: Voice of the customer/builder: a circular portrait + name/role on the left, a big pull-quote on the right. Humanizes a proof point — a real person said this.
patterns.when: Testimonial, voice-of-customer, builder spotlight, after a stats slide to put a face to the impact.
patterns.motion: vq-mark + vq-text reveal first (rise), portrait reveals slightly after
patterns.markup:
A
(or )
…
…
“
…key phrase…
patterns.pitfalls: Avatar is a circle (border-radius:50% + object-fit:cover for a real photo; letter fallback for none). Keep quote ≤2 sentences and bold ONE phrase. Attribute to a real person/role — a generic 'a builder said' reads as fabricated. Embed photo as base64 data-URI to keep the file self-contained.

patterns.intent: A process as connected left→right steps: numbered dots joined by a rail, each with title + body + an OUTPUT cue line. Reads as a pipeline where each stage produces something for the next.
patterns.when: Run-of-show, build pipeline, methodology phases, idea→ship flow. The horizontal sibling of the vertical timeline (use timeline for dates, stepper for stages).
patterns.motion: sp steps stagger-reveal left-to-right (rise, nth-child delays)
patterns.markup:
1
Title
body
Output: …
×N. Last step: .sp-line hidden.
patterns.proven_in: templates-v2 slide 4; reference-perspective slide 4 (Seed→Learn→Package→Scale, QA 0 errors)
patterns.pitfalls: Use align-items:flex-start (NOT stretch) so steps don't stretch full-height and float the output line to the slide bottom. CRITICAL for alignment: give .sp-b (description) a min-height (~66px = 3 lines) so the hairline + Output line align across ALL columns even when descriptions are different line-counts — otherwise a 3-line step pushes its output below the 2-line steps and the row looks ragged. Cap at 4-5 steps before columns get narrow. Connector line lives in .sp-rail next to the dot, hidden on last step.

patterns.intent: Walk a customer/user across the whole journey on one ultrawide canvas: stations on a spine left→right, an EMOTION/SENTIMENT CURVE plotted above, and the agent intervention pinned under each station. The width lets the WHOLE journey live on one slide — the signature 48:9 template.
patterns.when: 48:9 envisioning-theatre deck. As-is vs to-be customer journey, onboarding flow, service experience, 'where are you today → where you need to be'. The headline reason to use widescreen.
patterns.motion: static (or stagger stations + draw the emotion path with the motion engine)
patterns.markup: W.wjourney({kicker,title,subtitle, stations:[{phase,when,emo:0-100,doing,tone:'low|win|gold',assist:{icon,t}}]}) — emo drives the curve height; tone colours the dot.
patterns.proven_in: templates-wide journey fixture (QA 0 errors, visually approved)
patterns.pitfalls: Emotion SVG is authored at exact px (viewBox = TRACK_W × 150, preserveAspectRatio=none) so dots sit on the curve. Keep .st-doing min-height so every station's assist row aligns on one baseline. Don't straddle a screen seam (x=1280/2560) with a station label — stations sit in even columns so they fall clear.

patterns.intent: Show a real cross-actor process: actors as horizontal lanes (rows), time/phase as columns, work cards placed in the cell where they happen — handoffs read as the eye moves right. A true service blueprint that needs the width.
patterns.when: 48:9. Service blueprint, process map, who-does-what across a flow, RACI-in-motion, hand-off choreography.
patterns.motion: static (or reveal lanes top-to-bottom)
patterns.markup: W.wswim({kicker,title, cols:['Intake','Assessment',..], lanes:[{actor,icon,cls:'lane-cust|lane-front|lane-agent|lane-back', steps:[{col,span,t,b,accent}]}]}) — steps placed by grid-column.
patterns.pitfalls: Lane label gutter is fixed 200px; step cards placed by grid-column/span on a repeat(NC) track. Keep ≤4 lanes × ≤4 cols or cards get cramped. Lane tint = the .lane-* class on the row.

patterns.intent: A phased plan as a Gantt-feel grid: phase columns across the top, workstream rows down the left, bars spanning the phases they run in. The width turns a cramped 16:9 timeline into a readable plan.
patterns.when: 48:9. Multi-workstream rollout, engagement plan, program roadmap, anything with phases × workstreams.
patterns.motion: static (or grow bars left-to-right)
patterns.markup: W.wroad({kicker,title, phases:[{t,w}], rows:[{lbl, bars:[{col,span,cls:'b1|b2|b3|ghost',t}]}]})
patterns.proven_in: templates-wide slide 5 (Agent Boss 90-day rollout, QA 0 errors)
patterns.pitfalls: road-body uses justify-content:space-evenly so rows fill the vertical space (avoids the dead-bottom trap). Bars placed by grid-column/span on repeat(NP). 'ghost' = dashed not-yet/optional bar.

patterns.intent: An ascending capability ladder that literally climbs left→right across the width — each tier card steps higher than the last, with level tag, name, definition and a concrete example.
patterns.when: 48:9. Maturity model, crawl-walk-run, assisted→autonomous, adoption stages where the upward climb is the point.
patterns.motion: static (or rise tiers in sequence)
patterns.markup: W.wramp({kicker,title, rise:56, steps:[{lvl,t,b,x}]}) — rise = px each tier lifts above the previous.
patterns.pitfalls: Cards align-items:end + per-card margin-bottom = i*rise builds the staircase. Give .rstep a min-height and put the example (.rs-x) on margin-top:auto so all cards are equal height and the example pins to the bottom.

patterns.intent: Three acts, one per physical screen — the two bezel seams become the frame between panels. Today → the shift → where to. Each screen holds one beat of the argument.
patterns.when: 48:9. Three-part narrative, past/present/future, problem/insight/destination, any clean 3-act structure that wants to USE the 3-screen geometry.
patterns.motion: static (or reveal acts left-to-right)
patterns.markup: W.wtript({kicker,title, acts:[{n,t,b,foot,dark}]}) — set dark:true on the final act to anchor the right screen.
patterns.proven_in: templates-wide slide 7 (where are you today/where to be, QA 0 errors)
patterns.pitfalls: Exactly 3 acts (one per screen). Acts divided by hairline borders that fall ON the seams. Keep act body ≤ ~3 lines. The dark act reads as the 'destination' — use it last.

patterns.intent: One central hub with many labelled spokes spread across the FULL width — argues 'one agent, grounded in every surface'. The panorama lets 8+ sources breathe instead of cramming a tight wheel.
patterns.when: 48:9. Platform/ecosystem map, one-hub-many-sources, integration surface, 'connected to everything'.
patterns.motion: static (or draw spokes + pop nodes in sequence)
patterns.markup: W.wconstel({kicker,title, hub:'AGENT', nodes:[{t,b}]}) — nodes auto-spread on two tiers across spanX with a gentle arc.
patterns.proven_in: templates-wide slide 10 (8-surface platform, QA 0 errors, vision-approved)
patterns.pitfalls: SVG is a FULL-BLEED absolute overlay (3840×720, viewBox 1:1, preserveAspectRatio=none) so coords==px and nothing letterboxes; put the title BEFORE the .constel div (flex:0 0 auto) or the flex:1 svg pushes the title into the node zone. Nodes alternate two y-tiers so 8 labels never collide. Carry data-qa-ignore='overlap' (SVG <text> range-boxes false-positive the OVERLAP check).

patterns.intent: Several personas side by side across the width, each a card with frustrations (today) and gains (with agents). Envisioning workshops use the panorama to hold the whole cast at once.
patterns.when: 48:9. Persona wall, voice-of-customer set, stakeholder map, 'who we're designing for'.
patterns.motion: static (or reveal cards left-to-right)
patterns.markup: W.wpersona({kicker,title, personas:[{name,role,initials, sections:[{h,items:[],gain:true}]}]})
patterns.pitfalls: Give cards generous padding + section margins so they fill the vertical height (don't hug the top). 'gain' section recolours bullets green. 3-4 personas max before cards get narrow.

patterns.intent: One persona's day as a left-to-right time strip — segments from dawn to dusk (with a subtle time-of-day wash), each a moment + what the agent does. The width = the timeline of a day.
patterns.when: 48:9. Day-in-the-life, before/after a workday, 'a Tuesday with an agent in the loop', hour-by-hour scenario.
patterns.motion: static (or reveal segments left-to-right)
patterns.markup: W.wday({kicker,title, segments:[{time,moment,b,assist,icon}]}) — 5 segments tile dawn→dusk via nth-child washes.
patterns.pitfalls: Assist line pins to the bottom with margin-top:auto + top hairline so they align across segments. The time-of-day washes are nth-child(1..5) — tune if you use a different segment count.

patterns.intent: Inputs (left screen) → platform/core (center screen) → outcomes (right screen), with arrows between. The 3-screen geometry maps perfectly to a left-to-right value chain.
patterns.when: 48:9. How-it-works, data→platform→outcomes, sources→engine→results, any input/transform/output story.
patterns.motion: static (or flow items into the core)
patterns.markup: W.wchain({kicker,title, leftCap, inputs:[{icon,t}], core:{t,b}, rightCap, outputs:[{icon,t}]})
patterns.pitfalls: Grid MUST be 5 columns (1fr auto 1.15fr auto 1fr) because the markup emits inputs·arrow·core·arrow·outputs — a 3-col grid wraps the arrows to a 2nd row and overflows the slide (caught by qa-wide).

patterns.intent: A long row of KPI tiles (big number + QoQ delta + inline sparkline) that finally breathe on ultrawide — 5 across without cramping. The exec readout, panorama edition.
patterns.when: 48:9. Program scoreboard, business review, 'the numbers that moved', metrics wall.
patterns.motion: static (or count-up + draw sparklines)
patterns.markup: W.wribbon({kicker,title, perRow:5, kpis:[{l,n,d,dir:'up|down',color,spark:[..]}]}) — sparkline auto-normalises to its own min/max.
patterns.proven_in: templates-wide slide 6 (Agent Boss numbers, QA 0 errors)
patterns.pitfalls: 5 across is the sweet spot on 48:9; perRow wraps into rows beyond that. dir controls arrow + colour — set per metric (down can be GOOD, e.g. cycle time). Tile top-border = the metric colour.

patterns.intent: Three giant gradient numbers, one centred per physical screen, divided by soft seams. Three proof points that each deserve a whole screen.
patterns.when: 48:9. Three headline stats, proof slide, 'what changed' in three numbers.
patterns.motion: static (or count-up each figure)
patterns.markup: W.wbigtri({kicker,title, stats:[{n,l,tone:'gold|green'}]}) — exactly 3.
patterns.pitfalls: Exactly 3 stats (one per screen). Gradient text uses background-clip:text — renders fine in raster PNG/PDF but can drop out of a VECTOR pdf export, so build the share-PDF from the rendered PNGs (render-wide.js does this).

patterns.intent: The framing family for a 48:9 deck: a panoramic COVER (title locked to the left screen, cinematic field center+right), a SECTION divider, and a big STATEMENT centred across all three screens. Keeps critical text clear of the bezel seams.
patterns.when: 48:9. Opening slide, chapter breaks, and the one-big-idea beat of an envisioning-theatre deck.
patterns.motion: static (or stagger the cover meta)
patterns.markup: W.wcover({eyebrow,title,subtitle,meta:[{k,v}],img}) · W.wsection({num,eyebrow,title,subtitle}) · W.wstatement({eyebrow,title})
patterns.pitfalls: wcover title sits in a fixed 1180px left column so it never crosses seam 1 (x=1280). wstatement must set max-width:none on .wc-title (it inherits the cover's 1100px cap and otherwise wraps into a narrow centre column).

patterns.intent: Theatrical OPEN: two velvet curtain panels part from the centre seam outward, unveiling the title across the whole width. The signature cinematic open for an envisioning-theatre deck — the width IS the stage.
patterns.when: 48:9 motion deck. Opening slide, a big reveal, 'the show begins'. Needs motion:true.
patterns.motion: curtainL/curtainR (scaleX 1→0 from each outer edge, 2.2s, brief hold then part) + curtainRise (stage fades up behind)
patterns.markup: W.wcurtain({eyebrow,title,subtitle}) — deck.motion:true required so wide-motion.css + deck-engine load and the .active-gated animation re-fires on entry.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-demo slide 1 (frame-strip verified: panels part centre→out, title revealed)
patterns.pitfalls: Each panel is width:50.2% anchored to its outer edge with transform-origin at that edge, so scaleX(0) collapses it OFF-SCREEN (correct). cubic-bezier starts fast — add a 0%,18% hold in the keyframe so 'both panels closed' is readable before the part. Verify with cap-wide-motion.js frame strip, NOT a still (a still shows only the open end-state).

patterns.intent: Solari DEPARTURES BOARD: the title/number flips in cell-by-cell across the panorama, each character rotating in with a left→right stagger — like an airport board updating. Mechanical, premium, attention-grabbing.
patterns.when: 48:9 motion deck. A headline number/word reveal ('25+ AGENTS', a big stat, a one-word theme). Needs motion:true.
patterns.motion: flapIn (rotateX -90deg→0 per cell, .5s, staggered via --d at 0.09s steps)
patterns.markup: W.wflap({eyebrow,word,caption}) — word is split into .flap cells (spaces → .flap.sp gaps); each cell gets an incrementing --d.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-demo slide 2 (frame-strip verified: cells flip in L→R, '25+ AGENTS' assembles)
patterns.pitfalls: Keep the word short — each cell is min-width:64px, so a long phrase overflows the width. Cells are display:inline-grid in a flex .flapline; rotateX needs the cell's own perspective (it reads fine without explicit perspective at this size). Use for 1-3 words / a stat, not a sentence.

patterns.intent: A soft LIGHT BEAM travels left→right across the full width, lighting each item as it passes — the rest sit dim until the beam reaches them. Reveals a row of proof points sequentially, the width as a stage the light crosses.
patterns.when: 48:9 motion deck. A row of 3-5 stats/items you want revealed one-by-one with drama. Needs motion:true.
patterns.motion: spotSweep (a blurred radial beam translateX -820→3840, 2.6s) + spotLit per item (opacity .16→1 as the beam passes, staggered via --d)
patterns.markup: W.wspotlight({kicker,title, items:[{n,t,b,icon}]}) — each item's --d = i*0.42s so its lit moment matches the beam's arrival.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-demo slide 3 (frame-strip verified: visible beam, items light L→R)
patterns.pitfalls: Tune each item's --d to the beam timing (beam is 2.6s across 3840+820px; i*0.42s lands ~right). Items rest at opacity:.16 (dim, not invisible) so the un-lit state still reads. The beam is pointer-events:none and z-index:1 above the dim items.

patterns.intent: DAWN BREAKS: a luminous line splits at the centre and grows across the full width while a blue glow rises from it, then the headline fades in. A 'new era / the shift' beat — light spilling across all three screens.
patterns.when: 48:9 motion deck. The big thesis/shift statement, a 'dawn of X' moment, an emotional pivot. Needs motion:true.
patterns.motion: hlineGrow (line width 0→3400px, 1.6s) + hglowRise (radial glow scales/fades up, delay .5s) + .reveal headline (fades in, delay 1s)
patterns.markup: W.whorizon({eyebrow,title,attribution}) — title/eyebrow carry .reveal with inline animation-delay so they land after the glow.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-demo slide 4 (frame-strip verified after .reveal fix: line grows, glow rises, headline lands)
patterns.pitfalls: CRITICAL: .reveal must have its OWN reveal animation on .active (body.wide .slide.active .reveal{animation:wrise...}) — without it, .reveal only sets opacity:0 and the text NEVER appears (this exact bug shipped: glow looked great, headline was invisible). Inline animation-delay on the title staggers it after the glow.

patterns.intent: ALTARPIECE: the three act-panels swing open in 3D — left panel hinging from its left edge, right from its right, centre fading up — revealing a three-act narrative. The animated sibling of the static triptych.
patterns.when: 48:9 motion deck. A 3-act arc (today/shift/destination) you want to OPEN dramatically. Needs motion:true.
patterns.motion: unfoldL/unfoldR (perspective rotateY 78deg→0 from the outer hinge, 1.4s) + centre wrise (delay .5s)
patterns.markup: W.wunfold({kicker,title, acts:[{n,t,b,foot,dark}]}) — same data shape as wtript; first act gets .unfold-l, last .unfold-r, middle .unfold-c.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-demo slide 5 (frame-strip verified: panels resolve staggered, all 3 readable + flat at end)
patterns.pitfalls: Side panels need transform-origin at the OUTER edge (left panel: left center; right panel: right center) so they hinge like doors. End-state must be rotateY(0) + opacity:1 (flat + readable) — don't leave any residual rotation or the text stays skewed.

patterns.intent: The customer journey PERFORMS ITSELF: the emotion curve draws L→R, the spine grows across the width, station cards rise in a staggered wave, and each sentiment dot pops as the line reaches it. Motion = the narration; you talk through it as it builds.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. The hero journey slide when you want it to assemble live as you present. The same wjourney template — motion fires only when deck.motion:true.
patterns.motion: drawOn (emotion curve dash, 2s) + drawX (spine scaleX, 1.9s) + wrise per station (--d = 0.4+i*0.34s) + rideIn dots (--d timed to the curve reaching each x)
patterns.markup: W.wjourney({...}) in a motion:true deck — the curve <path> carries .drawpath pathLength=1, the spine .drawx, each station .wrise with staggered --d, each dot .ridein.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 7 (frame-strip verified: curve draws L→R, spine grows, 6 stations rise in wave, dots pop)
patterns.pitfalls: Hide-states (.wrise/.drawpath etc.) are scoped to body.wide.motion-on so the SAME template renders fully static in the gallery (no wide-motion.css) — verify the static gallery still shows all stations. Dot --d must roughly match when the curve's dash reaches that x (i/(N-1)*drawDur).

patterns.intent: An exec scoreboard that ASSEMBLES: the KPI tiles rise in a L→R wave, the big numbers count up from 0 to their value, and the delta + sparkline fade in last. The numbers literally climb as you reveal the slide.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. A 'numbers that moved' readout you want to land with the count-up drama. Same wribbon template; motion fires on deck.motion:true.
patterns.motion: wrise per tile (--d = 0.2+i*0.12s) + engine count-up (cubic ease, 1.3s, on pure integers) + delayed fade of delta (--dd) and sparkline (--ds)
patterns.markup: W.wribbon({...}) in motion:true — a plain-integer n (e.g. '25','90', optionally with +/% suffix) auto-becomes <span data-countup data-to=…>; non-integers like '−96%' stay static.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 9 (frame-strip verified: tiles rise L→R, 16→25 / 58→90 count up, sparklines fade in)
patterns.pitfalls: Only pure integers count up (regex /^\d{1,4}[+%]?$/) — '−96%' and 'hrs→mins' correctly render static. Count-up needs the deck-engine runCount hook (fires on show()). Tile --d wave must finish before the count starts feeling laggy — keep i*0.12s.

patterns.intent: The plan FILLS ITSELF IN: Gantt bars grow from their left edge along the phase axis, staggered row-by-row top→bottom. The roadmap builds as you walk the workstreams.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. A phased plan you want to assemble live. Same wroad template; motion on deck.motion:true.
patterns.motion: barGrow (scaleX 0→1 from left edge, 1s) staggered per bar (--d = 0.2 + row*0.18 + col*0.06s)
patterns.markup: W.wroad({...}) in motion:true — each .rr-bar gets a computed --d so bars grow in reading order (top-left first).
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 10 (frame-strip verified: bars grow L→R from left edge, staggered top→bottom)
patterns.pitfalls: transform-origin:left center on .rr-bar so bars grow rightward (not from centre). The ghost (dashed) bar grows too — fine. Stagger uses row+col so a wide bar doesn't outrun a later row.

patterns.intent: Vertical SHUTTER/BLINDS flip open in sequence across the width, unveiling the title behind. A crisp, mechanical alternative to the velvet curtain.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. An opening or section break with a sharper, more modern feel than the curtain.
patterns.motion: slatFlip per slat (rotateX 0→-92deg + fade, 1.3s, staggered --d=i*0.07s) + shutter-stage wrise (delay .9s)
patterns.markup: W.wshutter({eyebrow,title,subtitle}) — 12 .slat spans flip away; the .shutter-stage holds the content. motion:true required.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 2 (frame-strip verified: slats flip away staggered, title revealed)
patterns.pitfalls: Slats are dark-on-dark on a black theme — give them a slightly lighter gradient (#15151b) than the bg or the 'blinds' barely read. transform-origin:top center so they flip like horizontal venetian slats.

patterns.intent: A circular APERTURE opens from the centre (film iris-in), the scene growing out of a point to fill all three screens. Classic cinema reveal.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. A dramatic single-focal-point open ('a window into…', a portal).
patterns.motion: irisOpen (clip-path circle(0%→120% at centre), 1.7s)
patterns.markup: W.wiris({eyebrow,title,subtitle}) — the .iris layer carries the content + a radial glow; clip-path grows. motion:true.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 3 (frame-strip verified: small centre circle expands to full reveal)
patterns.pitfalls: clip-path:circle needs an explicit final radius ≥120% so the circle fully clears the 48:9 corners (a 100% radius leaves dark wedges at the far left/right corners on an ultrawide box).

patterns.intent: A headline TYPES ITSELF across the width with a blinking caret — like a terminal or a live transcript. For a quote, a question to the agent, or a thesis that assembles word by word.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. A statement you want to land as if typed live ('We ran this on ourselves first.').
patterns.motion: typeIn (inline-block width 0→100%, steps(40), 2.2s) + caretBlink (infinite) + .reveal attribution after
patterns.markup: W.wtypewriter({eyebrow,title,attribution}) — title wrapped in .typeline (overflow:hidden, white-space:nowrap) + a .caret. motion:true.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 6 (frame-strip verified: text types L→R, live blue caret, full sentence by end)
patterns.pitfalls: Keep the line to ONE row (white-space:nowrap) — a wrapping typeline reveals lower lines early. The steps() count should roughly match character count for a believable cadence. Width-based reveal (not a per-char JS) so it's cheap + smooth.

patterns.intent: The ECOSYSTEM ASSEMBLES ITSELF: a central hub pops in first, then spokes DRAW OUT from the hub one by one, and each node + label pops as its spoke lands. The harness/ecosystem builds from the centre outward.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. A hub-and-spoke (one core + 6-10 connected systems) you want to assemble live — perfect for an integration/harness/platform story. Same wconstel template; motion on deck.motion:true.
patterns.motion: hubPop (scale .2→1 spring, .7s) + spokeDraw per line (stroke-dash length→0 from the hub, staggered --d=0.5+i*0.16s) + ridein nodes/labels (pop after each spoke lands, --d+0.42s)
patterns.markup: W.wconstel({kicker,title,hub, nodes:[{t,b}]}) in motion:true — each spoke <line> carries .drawspoke with --len (computed Math.hypot), each node .ridein.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 14 (frame-strip verified: hub pops, 8 spokes draw outward, nodes pop in sequence)
patterns.pitfalls: Spoke draw uses stroke-dasharray:var(--len) + dashoffset:var(--len)→0 — the --len MUST be the line's pixel length (Math.hypot(dx,dy)) or the dash won't cover it. cn-node circles need transform-box:fill-box for the scale-pop to originate at the node, not the SVG origin. Keep this slide's data-qa-ignore='overlap' (SVG text glyph boxes false-positive).

patterns.intent: An anamorphic LENS FLARE streaks left→right across the panorama once on entry, then the content lights up behind it. Ambient, cinematic — a premium 'reveal by light' for a statement slide.
patterns.when: 48:9 MOTION deck. A statement/quote you want to enter with a cinematic flourish (softer than a curtain, more atmospheric than a fade).
patterns.motion: leakSweep (a blurred screen-blend streak translateX -1200→3900px, 2.2s, fades in/out at the ends) + .reveal content (delay .5s)
patterns.markup: W.wleak({eyebrow,title,attribution}) — a .leak span sweeps over the .wst-stage (which carries .reveal). motion:true.
patterns.proven_in: wide-motion-showcase slide 15 (frame-strip verified: bright streak moves L→R, headline lights up behind)
patterns.pitfalls: mix-blend-mode:screen makes the streak read as LIGHT (additive) not a grey bar — keep it. The streak is pointer-events:none, z-index above content. Time the content .reveal (delay .5s) so the headline appears as the flare passes over it, not before.

patterns.intent: Hard-left title on a charcoal field with a full-width brand-accent HORIZON rule and a photo bleeding in from the right. The signature opener for a co-branded customer deck.
patterns.when: 48:9 cover slide where you want the customer's brand color to own the room and an ambient product/scene image without a hard frame.
patterns.motion: static; horizon glow + image bleed via layered gradients
patterns.markup: W.wbrandcover({eyebrow,title,subtitle,img,meta:[{k,v}]}) — needs deck.brand for the accent; img dissolves via ::before/::after gradient masks (tune fade stops if a hard edge shows)
patterns.pitfalls: Dark source images need img opacity ~.7 + a multi-stop left gradient or a hard vertical edge appears. Keep title width <=17ch so it clears the image.

patterns.intent: A left-to-right agenda/journey as N icon-nodes pinned on a glowing brand-accent SPINE. Kills the 'empty agenda' void on a panoramic wall.
patterns.when: 48:9 agenda or multi-stage journey (4-7 stations) with times/owners. The fix when a centered bullet agenda looks barren across three screens.
patterns.motion: wrise stagger per station
patterns.markup: W.wtimeline({kicker,title,stations:[{time,icon,t,b,who,hot,product,chips:[productIcon...]}]}) — hot=glow the active station; chips render native product icons under it
patterns.pitfalls: Cap at ~6 stations or nodes crowd. chips must be product-icon names (p_*).

patterns.intent: Ascending steps (Intent->PoC->Wall->Transformation) with one step rendered as a tall dark WALL where progress stalls, and a glowing goal step beyond. Makes a 'scaling problem' visceral.
patterns.when: 48:9 maturity/why-now slide where the emotional point is that most efforts STALL at a barrier. Pressure beat.
patterns.motion: wrise stagger; steps lift via --lift
patterns.markup: W.wstair({kicker,title,subtitle,rise,steps:[{lvl,icon,t,b,wall:true,goal:true,tag}],seam}) — exactly one wall + one goal step
patterns.pitfalls: rise*N must stay within band; 4 steps @ rise 60 is the tested max.

patterns.intent: One enormous count-up NUMBER in the brand accent over a field of dim dots (a wall of stalled pilots), with a stark line beneath. The pressure PEAK.
patterns.when: 48:9 single-stat gut-punch ('$100M spent, zero in production'). Maximum drama, intentional negative space.
patterns.motion: data-countup animates the integer on reveal
patterns.markup: W.wgutpunch({eyebrow,figure:'$100M',line,quote,litDots:[i,...]}) — figure's digits auto-wrap in data-countup; STATIC fallback renders the real number (not 0) so PDF/QA are correct
patterns.pitfalls: The count-up span MUST render the real number as its text content, not '0', or static exports show 0. Geometry linter flags eyebrow/number overlap — benign.

patterns.intent: Three zones — left 'brings', a glowing CORE layer holding native product icons, right 'gets' — joined by widening flow arrows. Shows a value chain where the middle layer compounds.
patterns.when: 48:9 'better together'/value-chain slide where a central platform layer is the hinge and you want to SHOW its product surfaces, not just name them.
patterns.motion: static; arrow clip-paths
patterns.markup: W.wriver({kicker,title,leftCap,left:[{icon,t}],core:{t,icons:[p_*],b},rightCap,right:[{icon,t}],bridge})
patterns.pitfalls: core.icons should be 2 or 4 (renders a 2-col grid). Generic side icons tint; core icons stay native.

patterns.intent: A split canvas (e.g. Intelligence | Trust) divided by a brand-accent seam, each half a labelled set of pillar icon-cards. A clean two-column 'it comes down to two things' frame.
patterns.when: 48:9 slide contrasting/pairing two forces or pillar sets (3-4 items each). Also good for 'what EY brings | what clients still ask'.
patterns.motion: wrise per item
patterns.markup: W.wforces({kicker,title,forces:[{name,items:[{icon,t,b,product}]}],cap}) — exactly 2 forces; product:true keeps an icon native
patterns.pitfalls: Keep items balanced (same count each side) or the seam looks lopsided.

patterns.intent: A real hub-and-spoke: a central glowing hub + N native product-icon nodes on a gentle arc across the full width, pulsing spoke lines. The premium replacement for plain circle-node diagrams.
patterns.when: 48:9 'one X, four surfaces' slide (e.g. one agent grounded by four IQ products). When you must SHOW product icons as a system.
patterns.motion: hubflow dashed-spoke pulse; wrise nodes
patterns.markup: W.whub({kicker,title,hub,hubIcon,nodes:[{icon:p_*,t,b}]}) — geometry: fixed band H, cy=H/2, gentle arc (ry~120) so end-nodes stay level with the hub and clear the footer
patterns.pitfalls: Arc math is the trap: SVG viewBox must equal the band px height (preserveAspectRatio:none) so spokes meet nodes. Big vertical offsets push end-nodes off-canvas — keep ry modest and hub at band center. Re-run qa-wide after any geometry change.

patterns.intent: Two actor rails that MESH at column seams (teeth/zipper), with an optional joint-outcome strip along the bottom. The definitive 'who brings what' alliance slide — merges 2-3 weaker mapping slides into one.
patterns.when: 48:9 partnership/role-split across 3-4 columns where you want the two sides to read as interlocking, plus 'what the client sees'.
patterns.motion: wrise per cell
patterns.markup: W.wzipper({kicker,title,cols:[..],topActor,topIcon,top:[..],botActor,botIcon,botProduct,bot:[..],outcomeLabel,outcome:[..]})
patterns.pitfalls: cols/top/bot/outcome arrays must be equal length = NC.

patterns.intent: A fill-in-the-blank Mad-Lib across N connected cards, each with a prompt + a faint worked EXAMPLE, for a live co-creation/workshop activity. Makes the 'working slide' actually usable.
patterns.when: 48:9 co-creation/exercise slide where the room builds something live (a pitch, a plan) and you want a structured template + a model answer.
patterns.motion: wrise per card
patterns.markup: W.wpitchcanvas({kicker,title,cards:[{n,icon,prompt,example,product}],foot2})
patterns.pitfalls: Keep prompts short with a visible '___' blank; examples in brand-accent italic.
